huggingface.summary: TimesFM (Time Series Foundation Model) is a pretrained decoder-only model for time-series forecasting. This repository contains the **Transformers** port of the official TimesFM 2.5 PyTorch release. **Resources and Technical Documentation**: * Original model: google/timesfm-2.5-200m-pytorch * Paper: A decoder-only foundation model for time-series forecasting * Transformers docs: TimesFM 2.5 This model is converted from the official TimesFM 2.5 PyTorch checkpoint and integrated into `transformers` as `TimesFm2_5ModelForPrediction`. The converted checkpoint preserves the original architecture and forecasting behavior, including: * patch-based inputs for time-series contexts * decoder-only self-attention stack * point and quantile forecasts

papers.summary: Open-world navigation requires robots to make decisions in complex everyday environments while adapting to flexible task requirements. Conventional navigation approaches often rely on dense 3D reconstruction and hand-crafted goal metrics, which limits their generalization across tasks and environments. Recent advances in vision--language navigation (VLN) and vision--language--action (VLA) models enable end-to-end policies conditioned on natural language, but typically require interactive trainin
papers.summary_zh: 開放世界導航要求機器人在複雜的日常環境中做出決策，同時適應靈活的任務需求。傳統的導航方法通常依賴於密集的 3D 重建和手動設計的目標指標，這限制了它們在不同任務和環境中的泛化能力。最近在 vision--language navigation (VLN) 和 vision--language--action (VLA) 模型方面的進展，使得基於自然語言的端到端策略成為可能，但通常需要互動式訓練。
papers.title: OpenFrontier: General Navigation with Visual-Language Grounded Frontiers

papers.summary_zh: 基於知識的視覺問答 (KB-VQA) 要求模型透過整合外部知識來回答關於圖像的問題，由於嘈雜的檢索以及知識庫結構化、百科全書式的性質，這帶來了顯著的挑戰。這些特性使得與預訓練的 multimodal large language models (MLLMs) 之間產生了分佈差距，導致在訓練後階段難以進行有效的推理和領域適應。在這項工作中，我們提出了 	extit{Wiki-R1}，這是一種資料生成

primary_link.title: FedBCD:Communication-Efficient Accelerated Block Coordinate Gradient Descent for Federated Learning

primary_link.title: CLIP-driven Zero-shot Learning with Ambiguous Labels

papers.summary: Zero-shot learning (ZSL) aims to recognize unseen classes by leveraging semantic information from seen classes, but most existing methods assume accurate class labels for training instances. However, in real-world scenarios, noise and ambiguous labels can significantly reduce the performance of ZSL. papers.summary_zh: Zero-shot learning (ZSL) 旨在透過利用已知類別的語義資訊來識別未知類別，但大多數現有方法都假設訓練實例具有準確的類別標籤。然而，在實際場景中，雜訊和歧義標籤會顯著降低 ZSL 的性能。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了一個新的 CLIP-driven partial label zero-shot learning (CLIP-PZSL) 框架來處理標籤歧義。首先，我們使用 CLIP 提取實例和標籤特徵。然後，一個語義

papers.title_zh: CLIP 驅動的帶有歧義標籤的 Zero-shot Learning

links.title: Non-Euclidean Gradient Descent Operates at the Edge of Stability

primary_link.title: Non-Euclidean Gradient Descent Operates at the Edge of Stability

papers.summary: The Edge of Stability (EoS) is a phenomenon where the sharpness (largest eigenvalue) of the Hessian converges to $2/η$ during training with gradient descent (GD) with a step-size $η$. Despite (apparently) violating classical smoothness assumptions, EoS has been widely observed in deep learning, but its theoretical foundations remain incomplete. We provide an interpretation of EoS through the lens of Directional Smoothness Mishkin et al. [2024]. This interpretation naturally extends to non-Euclid
papers.summary_zh: 穩定性邊緣 (EoS) 是一種現象，其中在以步長 $η$ 進行 gradient descent (GD) 訓練期間，Hessian 的銳度（最大特徵值）收斂到 $2/η$。儘管（顯然）違反了經典的平滑性假設，EoS 在 deep learning 中已被廣泛觀察到，但其理論基礎仍不完整。我們透過 Directional Smoothness Mishkin et al. [2024] 的視角提供了對 EoS 的解釋。這種解釋自然地擴展到非歐幾里德
papers.title: Non-Euclidean Gradient Descent Operates at the Edge of Stability
papers.title_zh: 非歐幾里德梯度下降在穩定性邊緣運作

links.title: Latent Wasserstein Adversarial Imitation Learning

primary_link.title: Latent Wasserstein Adversarial Imitation Learning

papers.summary: Imitation Learning (IL) enables agents to mimic expert behavior by learning from demonstrations. However, traditional IL methods require large amounts of medium-to-high-quality demonstrations as well as actions of expert demonstrations, both of which are often unavailable. To reduce this need, we propose Latent Wasserstein Adversarial Imitation Learning (LWAIL), a novel adversarial imitation learning framework that focuses on state-only distribution matching. It benefits from the Wasserstein dis
papers.summary_zh: Imitation Learning (IL) 使代理能夠透過從示範中學習來模仿專家行為。然而，傳統的 IL 方法需要大量中高品質的示範以及專家示範的動作，這兩者往往都難以取得。為了減少這種需求，我們提出 Latent Wasserstein Adversarial Imitation Learning (LWAIL)，這是一個新穎的 adversarial imitation learning 框架，專注於僅狀態分佈匹配。它受益於 Wasserstein 距離的特性。
papers.title: Latent Wasserstein Adversarial Imitation Learning
papers.title_zh: 潛在 Wasserstein 對抗式模仿學習

primary_link.title: An Exploration-Analysis-Disambiguation Reasoning Framework for Word Sense Disambiguation with Low-Parameter LLMs

papers.summary: Word Sense Disambiguation (WSD) remains a key challenge in Natural Language Processing (NLP), especially when dealing with rare or domain-specific senses that are often misinterpreted. While modern high-parameter Large Language Models (LLMs) such as GPT-4-Turbo have shown state-of-the-art WSD performance, their computational and energy demands limit scalability. This study investigates whether low-parameter LLMs (<4B parameters) can achieve comparable results through fine-tuning strategies that
papers.summary_zh: Word Sense Disambiguation (WSD) 仍然是 Natural Language Processing (NLP) 中的一個關鍵挑戰，尤其是在處理經常被誤解的稀有或領域特定詞義時。儘管 GPT-4-Turbo 等現代高參數 Large Language Models (LLMs) 在 WSD 方面展現出 state-of-the-art 的性能，但其計算和能源需求限制了其可擴展性。本研究探討了低參數 LLMs (<4B parameters) 是否能透過 fine-tuning 策略達到可媲美的結果。
papers.title: An Exploration-Analysis-Disambiguation Reasoning Framework for Word Sense Disambiguation with Low-Parameter LLMs
papers.title_zh: 一種用於低參數 LLM 的詞義消歧探索-分析-消歧推理框架

links.title: WavSLM: Single-Stream Speech Language Modeling via WavLM Distillation

primary_link.title: WavSLM: Single-Stream Speech Language Modeling via WavLM Distillation

papers.summary: Large language models show that simple autoregressive training can yield scalable and coherent generation, but extending this paradigm to speech remains challenging due to the entanglement of semantic and acoustic information. Most existing speech language models rely on text supervision, hierarchical token streams, or complex hybrid architectures, departing from the single-stream generative pretraining paradigm that has proven effective in text. In this work, we introduce WavSLM, a speech langu
papers.summary_zh: Large language models 表明，簡單的 autoregressive 訓練可以產生可擴展且連貫的生成內容，但由於語義和聲學資訊的糾纏，將此範式擴展到語音仍具挑戰性。大多數現有的 speech language models 依賴於文本監督、分層 token 串流或複雜的混合架構，偏離了在文本中證明有效的 single-stream generative pretraining 範式。在這項工作中，我們介紹了 WavSLM，一種 speech language 模型。
papers.title: WavSLM: Single-Stream Speech Language Modeling via WavLM Distillation
papers.title_zh: WavSLM：透過 WavLM 蒸餾實現單一串流語音語言建模

links.title: A framework for assessing the capabilities of code generation of constraint domain-specific languages with large language models

primary_link.title: A framework for assessing the capabilities of code generation of constraint domain-specific languages with large language models

papers.summary: Large language models (LLMs) can be used to support software development tasks, e.g., through code completion or code generation. However, their effectiveness drops significantly when considering less popular programming languages such as domain-specific languages (DSLs). In this paper, we propose a generic framework for evaluating the capabilities of LLMs generating DSL code from textual specifications. The generated code is assessed from the perspectives of well-formedness and correctness. Thi
papers.summary_zh: Large language models (LLMs) 可用於支援軟體開發任務，例如透過 code completion 或 code generation。然而，當考慮到較不流行的程式語言，例如 domain-specific languages (DSLs) 時，它們的有效性會顯著下降。在本文中，我們提出了一個通用框架，用於評估 LLMs 從文本規範生成 DSL code 的能力。生成的 code 從 well-formedness 和 correctness 的角度進行評估。
papers.title: A framework for assessing the capabilities of code generation of constraint domain-specific languages with large language models
papers.title_zh: 一種評估大型語言模型生成受限領域特定語言程式碼能力的框架

links.title: Reclaiming Lost Text Layers for Source-Free Cross-Domain Few-Shot Learning

primary_link.title: Reclaiming Lost Text Layers for Source-Free Cross-Domain Few-Shot Learning

papers.summary: Source-Free Cross-Domain Few-Shot Learning (SF-CDFSL) focuses on fine-tuning with limited training data from target domains (e.g., medical or satellite images), where CLIP has recently shown promising results due to its generalizability to downstream tasks. Current works indicate CLIP's text encoder is more suitable for cross-domain tasks, however, we find that \textbf{removing certain middle layers of the text encoder can effectively improve performance in SF-CDFSL}, which we call the Lost Laye
papers.summary_zh: Source-Free Cross-Domain Few-Shot Learning (SF-CDFSL) 專注於使用來自目標領域（例如醫學或衛星圖像）的有限訓練數據進行 fine-tuning，其中 CLIP 最近因其對 downstream tasks 的泛化能力而顯示出有前景的結果。目前的研究表明 CLIP 的 text encoder 更適用於 cross-domain tasks，然而，我們發現 移除 text encoder 的某些中間層可以有效提高 SF-CDFSL 的性能，我們將其稱為 Lost Layer。
papers.title: Reclaiming Lost Text Layers for Source-Free Cross-Domain Few-Shot Learning
papers.title_zh: 為無源跨領域少樣本學習找回遺失的文本層

links.title: Lifelong Language-Conditioned Robotic Manipulation Learning

primary_link.title: Lifelong Language-Conditioned Robotic Manipulation Learning

papers.summary: Traditional language-conditioned manipulation agent sequential adaptation to new manipulation skills leads to catastrophic forgetting of old skills, limiting dynamic scene practical deployment. In this paper, we propose SkillsCrafter, a novel robotic manipulation framework designed to continually learn multiple skills while reducing catastrophic forgetting of old skills. Specifically, we propose a Manipulation Skills Adaptation to retain the old skills knowledge while inheriting the shared knowl
papers.summary_zh: 傳統的 language-conditioned manipulation agent 對於新操作技能的順序適應，會導致對舊技能的 catastrophic forgetting，限制了其在動態場景中的實際部署。在本文中，我們提出 SkillsCrafter，這是一種新穎的 robotic manipulation framework，旨在持續學習多種技能，同時減少對舊技能的 catastrophic forgetting。具體來說，我們提出了一種 Manipulation Skills Adaptation 方法，以保留舊技能知識並繼承共享知識。
papers.title: Lifelong Language-Conditioned Robotic Manipulation Learning
papers.title_zh: 終身語言條件化機器人操作學習

links.title: MedCoRAG: Interpretable Hepatology Diagnosis via Hybrid Evidence Retrieval and Multispecialty Consensus

primary_link.title: MedCoRAG: Interpretable Hepatology Diagnosis via Hybrid Evidence Retrieval and Multispecialty Consensus

papers.summary: Diagnosing hepatic diseases accurately and interpretably is critical, yet it remains challenging in real-world clinical settings. Existing AI approaches for clinical diagnosis often lack transparency, structured reasoning, and deployability. Recent efforts have leveraged large language models (LLMs), retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), and multi-agent collaboration. However, these approaches typically retrieve evidence from a single source and fail to support iterative, role-specialized delibe
papers.summary_zh: 準確且可解釋地診斷肝臟疾病至關重要，但在現實世界的臨床環境中仍然充滿挑戰。現有的用於臨床診斷的 AI 方法通常缺乏透明度、結構化推理和可部署性。最近的研究利用了 large language models (LLMs)、retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) 和 multi-agent collaboration。然而，這些方法通常從單一來源檢索證據，並且未能支持迭代的、角色專業化的審議。

papers.title_zh: MedCoRAG：透過混合證據檢索與多專科共識實現可解釋的肝臟學診斷

links.title: Measuring the Redundancy of Decoder Layers in SpeechLLMs

primary_link.title: Measuring the Redundancy of Decoder Layers in SpeechLLMs

papers.summary: Speech Large Language Models route speech encoder representations into an LLM decoder that typically accounts for over 90% of total parameters. We study how much of this decoder capacity is actually needed for speech tasks. Across two LLM families and three scales (1-8B), we show that decoder redundancy is largely inherited from the pretrained LLM: text and speech inputs yield similar redundant blocks. We then measure excess capacity by pruning decoder layers and analysing post-pruning healing t
papers.summary_zh: Speech Large Language Models 將語音編碼器表示 (speech encoder representations) 路由到 LLM 解碼器中，該解碼器通常佔總參數的 90% 以上。我們研究了語音任務實際需要多少這種解碼器容量。橫跨兩個 LLM 家族和三種規模 (1-8B)，我們證明解碼器冗餘性主要繼承自預訓練的 LLM：文本和語音輸入產生相似的冗餘塊 (redundant blocks)。然後，我們通過剪枝解碼器層並分析剪枝後的恢復。
papers.title: Measuring the Redundancy of Decoder Layers in SpeechLLMs
papers.title_zh: 測量 SpeechLLMs 中解碼器層的冗餘性

links.title: ARC-TGI: Human-Validated Task Generators with Reasoning Chain Templates for ARC-AGI

primary_link.title: ARC-TGI: Human-Validated Task Generators with Reasoning Chain Templates for ARC-AGI

papers.summary: The Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus (ARC-AGI) probes few-shot abstraction and rule induction on small visual grids, but progress is difficult to measure on static collections of hand-authored puzzles due to overfitting, dataset leakage, and memorisation. We introduce ARC-TGI (ARC Task Generators Inventory), an open-source framework for task-family generators: compact Python programs that sample diverse ARC-AGI tasks while preserving a latent rule. ARC-TGI is built around a solver-facing represe
papers.summary_zh: Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus (ARC-AGI) 探索在小型視覺網格上的 few-shot 抽象和規則歸納，但由於 overfitting、dataset leakage 和 memorisation，在靜態的手工編寫謎題集合上很難衡量進度。我們介紹 ARC-TGI (ARC Task Generators Inventory)，這是一個用於任務家族生成器的開源框架：緊湊的 Python 程式，可在保留潛在規則的同時採樣多樣的 ARC-AGI 任務。ARC-TGI 圍繞著一個面向求解器的表示。
papers.title: ARC-TGI: Human-Validated Task Generators with Reasoning Chain Templates for ARC-AGI
papers.title_zh: ARC-TGI：用於 ARC-AGI 的帶有推理鏈模板且經人類驗證的任務生成器

primary_link.title: Poisoning the Inner Prediction Logic of Graph Neural Networks for Clean-Label Backdoor Attacks

papers.summary: Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) have achieved remarkable results in various tasks. Recent studies reveal that graph backdoor attacks can poison the GNN model to predict test nodes with triggers attached as the target class. However, apart from injecting triggers to training nodes, these graph backdoor attacks generally require altering the labels of trigger-attached training nodes into the target class, which is impractical in real-world scenarios. In this work, we focus on the clean-label graph ba
papers.summary_zh: Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) 在各種任務中取得了顯著的成果。最近的研究揭示，graph backdoor attacks 可以毒害 GNN 模型，使其將附帶 trigger 的測試節點預測為目標類別。然而，除了向訓練節點注入 trigger 之外，這些 graph backdoor attacks 通常還需要將附帶 trigger 的訓練節點的標籤更改為目標類別，這在現實世界場景中是不切實際的。在這項工作中，我們專注於 clean-label 圖後門攻擊。
papers.title: Poisoning the Inner Prediction Logic of Graph Neural Networks for Clean-Label Backdoor Attacks
papers.title_zh: 毒害圖神經網路的內部預測邏輯以實現 Clean-Label 後門攻擊

links.title: Cyber Threat Intelligence for Artificial Intelligence Systems

primary_link.title: Cyber Threat Intelligence for Artificial Intelligence Systems

radar.summary: As artificial intelligence (AI) becomes deeply embedded in critical services and everyday products, it is increasingly exposed to security threats which traditional cyber defenses were not designed to handle. In this paper, we investigate how cyber threat intelligence (CTI) may evolve to address attacks that target AI systems. radar.summary_zh: 隨著 Artificial Intelligence (AI) 深度融入關鍵服務和日常產品，它日益暴露於傳統網路防禦 (cyber defenses) 未能有效應對的安全威脅之中。在本文中，我們探討 Cyber Threat Intelligence (CTI) 如何演進以應對針對 AI 系統的攻擊。我們首先分析了 conventional threat intelligence 的假設和工作流程，並結合以 AI 為中心的防禦需求，突出了 AI 特有的資產和脆弱性...

links.title: Transducing Language Models

primary_link.title: Transducing Language Models

radar.summary: Modern language models define distributions over strings, but downstream tasks often require different output formats. For instance, a model that generates byte-pair strings does not directly produce word-level predictions, and a DNA model does not directly produce amino-acid sequences. radar.summary_zh: 現代語言模型定義了字串的分佈，但下游任務通常需要不同的輸出格式。例如，一個生成 byte-pair strings 的模型無法直接產生字詞級預測，而一個 DNA 模型也無法直接產生胺基酸序列。在這些情況下，確定性的字串到字串轉換可以將模型的輸出轉換為所需形式。這在機率論中是一個熟悉的模式：將函數 $f$ 應用於隨機變數 $X$

links.title: KARL: Knowledge Agents via Reinforcement Learning

primary_link.title: KARL: Knowledge Agents via Reinforcement Learning

top5.summary: We present a system for training enterprise search agents via reinforcement learning that achieves state-of-the-art performance across a diverse suite of hard-to-verify agentic search tasks. Our work makes four core contributions. First, we introduce KARLBench, a multi-capability evaluation suite spanning six distinct search regimes, including constraint-driven entity search, cross-document report synthesis, tabular numerical reasoning, exhaustive entity retrieval, procedural reasoning over technical documentation, and fact aggregation over internal enterprise notes. Second, we show that models trained across heterogeneous search behaviors generalize substantially better than those optimized for any single benchmark. Third, we develop an agentic synthesis pipeline that employs long-horizon reasoning and tool use to generate diverse, grounded, and high-quality training data, with iterative bootstrapping from increasingly capable models. Fourth, we propose a new post-training paradigm based on iterative large-batch off-policy RL that is sample efficient, robust to train-inference engine discrepancies, and naturally extends to multi-task training with out-of-distribution generalization. Compared to Claude 4.6 and GPT 5.2, KARL is Pareto-optimal on KARLBench across cost-quality and latency-quality trade-offs, including tasks that were out-of-distribution during training. With sufficient test-time compute, it surpasses the strongest closed models. These results show that tailored synthetic data in combination with multi-task reinforcement learning enables cost-efficient and high-performing knowledge agents for grounded reasoning.
top5.summary_zh: 我們提出一個系統，旨在透過 reinforcement learning 訓練企業搜尋 agent，該系統在各種難以驗證的 agentic 搜尋任務上實現了 state-of-the-art 的表現。我們的研究有四項核心貢獻。首先，我們推出了 KARLBench，這是一個多功能評估套件，涵蓋六種不同的搜尋範疇，包括 constraint-driven entity search、cross-document report synthesis、tabular numerical reasoning、exhaustive entity retrieval、technical documentation 上的 procedural reasoning 以及內部企業筆記中的 fact aggregation。其次，我們展示了經過異構搜尋行為訓練的模型，其泛化能力顯著優於僅針對單一 benchmark 進行優化的模型。第三，我們開發了一個 agentic synthesis pipeline，該 pipeline 採用 long-horizon reasoning 和 tool use 來生成多樣化、有根據且高品質的訓練數據，並透過越來越強大的模型進行 iterative bootstrapping。第四，我們提出了一種新的 post-training 範式，該範式基於 iterative large-batch off-policy RL，具有 sample efficient、對 train-inference engine 差異 robust 的特性，並且自然地擴展到具有 out-of-distribution generalization 的 multi-task training。與 Claude 4.6 和 GPT 5.2 相比，KARL 在 KARLBench 上於 cost-quality 和 latency-quality 的權衡方面達到 Pareto-optimal，包括在訓練期間屬於 out-of-distribution 的任務。在足夠的 test-time compute 下，它超越了最強的 closed models。這些結果表明，量身定制的 synthetic data 結合 multi-task reinforcement learning，能夠實現用於 grounded reasoning 的 cost-efficient 且高性能的 knowledge agents。

links.title: Towards Multimodal Lifelong Understanding: A Dataset and Agentic Baseline

primary_link.title: Towards Multimodal Lifelong Understanding: A Dataset and Agentic Baseline

top5.summary: While datasets for video understanding have scaled to hour-long durations, they typically consist of densely concatenated clips that differ from natural, unscripted daily life. To bridge this gap, we introduce MM-Lifelong, a dataset designed for Multimodal Lifelong Understanding. Comprising 181.1 hours of footage, it is structured across Day, Week, and Month scales to capture varying temporal densities. Extensive evaluations reveal two critical failure modes in current paradigms: end-to-end MLLMs suffer from a Working Memory Bottleneck due to context saturation, while representative agentic baselines experience Global Localization Collapse when navigating sparse, month-long timelines. To address this, we propose the Recursive Multimodal Agent (ReMA), which employs dynamic memory management to iteratively update a recursive belief state, significantly outperforming existing methods. Finally, we establish dataset splits designed to isolate temporal and domain biases, providing a rigorous foundation for future research in supervised learning and out-of-distribution generalization.
top5.summary_zh: 儘管用於 video understanding 的數據集已擴展到數小時長，但它們通常由密集拼接的片段組成，這與自然的、無劇本的日常生活不同。為了彌補這一鴻溝，我們推出了 MM-Lifelong，這是一個專為 Multimodal Lifelong Understanding 設計的數據集。它包含 181.1 小時的 footage，並以日、週、月為尺度進行結構化，以捕捉不同的時間密度。大量評估揭示了當前範式中的兩種關鍵失敗模式：end-to-end MLLMs 因 context saturation 而遭受 Working Memory Bottleneck，而具代表性的 agentic baselines 在導航稀疏的、長達數月的時間線時會遇到 Global Localization Collapse。為了應對此問題，我們提出了 Recursive Multimodal Agent (ReMA)，它採用 dynamic memory management 來迭代更新 recursive belief state，顯著優於現有方法。最後，我們建立了旨在隔離 temporal 和 domain biases 的 dataset splits，為未來在 supervised learning 和 out-of-distribution generalization 方面的研究提供了嚴謹的基礎。
top5.title: Towards Multimodal Lifelong Understanding: A Dataset and Agentic Baseline

links.title: On-Policy Self-Distillation for Reasoning Compression

primary_link.title: On-Policy Self-Distillation for Reasoning Compression

top5.summary:
Reasoning models think out loud, but much of what they say is noise. We introduce OPSDC (On-Policy Self-Distillation for Reasoning Compression), a method that teaches models to reason more concisely by
  distilling their own concise behavior back into themselves. The entire approach reduces to one idea: condition the same model on a "be concise" instruction to obtain teacher logits, and minimize per-token
  reverse KL on the student's own rollouts. No ground-truth answers, no token budgets, no difficulty estimators. Just self-distillation. Yet this simplicity belies surprising sophistication: OPSDC automatically
  compresses easy problems aggressively while preserving the deliberation needed for hard ones. On Qwen3-8B and Qwen3-14B, we achieve 57-59% token reduction on MATH-500 while improving accuracy by 9-16 points
  absolute. On AIME 2024, the 14B model gains 10 points with 41% compression. The secret? Much of what reasoning models produce is not just redundant-it is actively harmful, compounding errors with every
  unnecessary token.
top5.summary_zh: Reasoning 模型會把思考過程外顯出來，但它們所說的很多內容都是噪音。我們介紹了 OPSDC (On-Policy Self-Distillation for Reasoning Compression)，這是一種透過將模型自身的簡潔行為 distillation 回模型本身，來教會模型更簡潔地進行 reasoning 的方法。整個方法歸結為一個核心思想：在「be concise」指令下對同一模型進行 condition 以獲取 teacher logits，並在 student 自己的 rollouts 上最小化 per-token reverse KL。沒有 ground-truth 答案，沒有 token budgets，沒有 difficulty estimators。僅僅是 self-distillation。然而，這種簡潔性卻掩蓋了令人驚訝的精妙之處：OPSDC 會積極壓縮簡單問題，同時保留處理困難問題所需的深思熟慮。在 Qwen3-8B 和 Qwen3-14B 上，我們在 MATH-500 上實現了 57-59% 的 token 減少，同時將準確度提高了 9-16 個絕對百分點。在 AIME 2024 上，14B 模型在 41% 的壓縮下提高了 10 個百分點。秘訣是什麼？reasoning 模型產生的大部分內容不僅是冗餘的，而且是有害的，每個不必要的 token 都會加劇錯誤。
top5.title: On-Policy Self-Distillation for Reasoning Compression
top5.title_zh: 用於推理壓縮的 On-Policy Self-Distillation
